_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Local FL batch: 1 Melbourne CC (LaRusso) + 4 Miami (King, Pardo, Higgins, Gonzalez). Two CURRENCY CORRECTIONS: (1) Gonzalez LOST the Dec 9 2025 mayoral runoff to Higgins 40.5-59.5 — set status/candidacy lost; existing record falsely showed 'won' and credited his USCIS service to Trump (was Bush 2005-2008). (2) Higgins is now sitting Mayor of Miami (sworn Dec 18 2025, term to 2029), not still a county commissioner. The June-2025 Miami City Commission vote to postpone the Nov-2025 election was struck down by the FL 3rd DCA Aug 2025 and the election proceeded; King won re-election Nov 2025 84.4%; Pardo SPONSORED the postponement (yes-vote) — context goes in notes, scored against specific rubric cells only. All FALSE/TRUE cells cited; vague slogans and party-default presumptions held to null.

profile.background: Owner/founder, Paper & Plastics Direct, LLC (Melbourne foodservice-disposables distributor, est. 2010). Melbourne City Council District 2 first elected 2004, served two terms through 2012 (incl. stints as vice mayor); returned to D2 Nov 2018, re-elected Nov 2022 with 68.67%; served as Vice Mayor Nov 2022-Nov 2023. Sits on Melbourne Orlando International Airport Authority and as alternate on Space Coast TPO. Council seat is non-partisan but he is a registered Republican.

mark-larusso.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement evidence-pass] Long-tenured D2 council member with an exceptionally thin culture-war public footprint. HARD evidence points: (a) 2012 Brevard FOP Lodge 37 endorsement with affirmative pro-LE quote ('I have always valued the important job performed by all members of the law enforcement community') — public_justice q0 TRUE; (b) yes-vote on Melbourne FY2025-26 budget setting operating millage at 7.0112 (11.4% above rollback) — economic_stewardship q2 FALSE; (c) personal R registration. Did not return Hometown News candidate questionnaires in 2018 or 2022; @malarusso X account is sparse and non-political. NO EVIDENCE on abortion, marriage/sexuality, school curriculum, election admin, immigration, 2A — held null per evidence-first discipline.

2.note: Voted aye on Melbourne FY2025-26 budget setting operating millage at 7.0112 mills (11.4% above rollback); no affirmative anti-debt advocacy on record.

0.note: Brevard FOP Lodge 37 endorsement; his own statement: 'I have always valued the important job performed by all members of the law enforcement community.' Meets the affirmative-advocacy bar.

profile.background: Born Guyana, raised Miami. JD, Nova Southeastern. 18-year Miami-Dade County career incl. Chief of Constituent Services for a County Commissioner and exec at Community Action Agency; former President/CEO, Martin Luther King Economic Development Corp. First Guyanese-American elected to City of Miami; first woman to chair the Miami City Commission. Took office Nov 10 2021; re-elected Nov 4 2025 with 84.4% over Marion Brown and Frederick Bryant; sworn in Nov 20 2025 for second and final four-year term ending ~2029. District 5 covers Liberty City, Little Haiti, Model City, Overtown, Wynwood/Edgewater, Upper East Side.

christine-king.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement evidence-pass] Re-elected Nov 4 2025 with 84.4%; second four-year term to ~2029. TWO DEFINING VOTES: (1) JUNE 2025: voted YES in the 3-2 ordinance to postpone Miami's Nov 2025 city election by a year, extending her own term; FL 3rd DCA struck it down as unconstitutional Aug 2025 and the election proceeded — captured in NOTES not rubric q4 (Zuckerbucks-specific). (2) JUNE 2025: voted NO on Miami's 287(g) ICE partnership, explicitly as 'an immigrant... I do not volunteer' — refuse_state_overreach q0 TRUE (asserted local control against the DeSantis/Uthmeier state sanctuary-ban posture) AND border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE (functionally pro-sanctuary). Platform names 'gun violence reduction' framed in housing/youth/mental-health terms — public_justice q3 FALSE (treats violent crime in public-health frame). 'First responder support' campaign-site language is generic boilerplate that does not meet the affirmative-advocacy HIGH BAR for public_justice q0 — held null. No documented record on abortion, marriage/sexuality, school curriculum, paper ballots, voter ID, 2A — null.

3.note: Campaign platform's top priority is 'gun violence reduction' framed in community/public-health terms (housing, youth programs, mental health) — consistent with public-health framing of violent crime.

0.note: Voted NO on Miami's 287(g) ICE agreement (June 2025) over FL AG Uthmeier's threats that the state's sanctuary-cities ban required compliance — explicit assertion of local control against the state mandate.

1.note: Voted NO on 287(g) police-ICE deputization; quoted opposing the state's enforcement posture ('It's voluntary, and I do not volunteer').

2.note: Voted NO on 287(g) — functionally pro-sanctuary posture; quoted: 'I feel the pain of my country; I feel the pain of my residents afraid to leave their homes...'

profile.background: Financial advisor; longtime LGBTQ-rights activist. Founding Chair of SAVE (Safeguarding American Values for Everyone, aka SAVE Dade) in 1993 — the org that successfully lobbied to restore Miami-Dade's gay-rights anti-discrimination ordinance in 1998 after the 1977 Anita Bryant repeal. Co-founded 4Ward Miami (Gay8 Festival); led CareResource (AIDS service org); served on Miami-Dade Gay & Lesbian Chamber. Raised Catholic (St. Hugh + Belen Jesuit Prep); current faith identity not publicly documented. Nov 21 2023 won runoff vs. Sabina Covo; sworn in Dec 2 2023 to 4-year term ending 2027 (postponement that would have pushed term to 2028 was struck down by FL 3rd DCA Aug 2025); first openly gay person elected to Miami City Commission.

damian-pardo.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement evidence-pass] First openly gay Miami City Commissioner (Nov 2023). SAVE Dade founding Chair (1993) — direct documentary evidence of LGBTQ-rights advocacy supplies all three biblical_marriage cells as FALSE on his OWN public record (not party inference). Publicly opposed FL HB 1557 ('Don't Say Gay'), so family_child_sovereignty q1/q2 FALSE. JUNE 2025: SPONSORED the 3-2 ordinance to delay Miami's city election to 2026, extending his own term; FL 3rd DCA reversed Aug 2025, election proceeded (Pardo's own D2 seat was not up). JUNE 2025: voted NO on Miami's 287(g) ICE partnership, quoted invoking local-control / 'not our brand' language — refuse_state_overreach q0/q2/q4 TRUE; border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE (pro-sanctuary). Voted for FY2025-26 budget that funds a 300-officer expansion — note in record; does NOT meet the affirmative-advocacy HIGH BAR for public_justice q0 (per skill: routine budget yes ≠ credit), held null. No public statements located on abortion, Christian liberty, paper ballots/voter ID/Zuckerbucks specifically, or 2A — null.

0.note: Founding Chair of SAVE Dade (1993-), the Miami-Dade LGBTQ-rights org; openly gay; first openly gay Miami City Commissioner. Built his civic career advocating against the one-man-one-woman marriage framework.

2.note: Sits on Miami's LGBTQ Advisory Board (which explicitly includes trans representation); publicly opposed FL HB 1557; no statement rejecting trans ideology.

4.note: Founder of SAVE Dade (lobbies for pro-LGBTQ public policy) and 4Ward Miami / Gay8 Festival (public LGBTQ celebration); on-record opposing 'Don't Say Gay.'

1.note: Public opposition to FL HB 1557 (the parental-notification framework on gender topics in K-12).

2.note: On record opposing HB 1557 (the FL law restricting K-12 SOGI instruction).

0.note: Voted NO on Miami's 287(g) ICE agreement, explicitly invoking local-control / 'not our brand' language — asserted local autonomy against the state-mandated cooperation posture (content-neutral on direction).

2.note: Same 287(g) NO vote protected immigrant residents from state-mandated federal-enforcement deputization.

4.note: Refused to participate in 287(g) deputization despite FL AG Uthmeier's threat that the state's sanctuary ban required participation.

1.note: Voted NO on 287(g) ICE police deputization; quoted opposing 'fear, repression, and hostility' around immigration enforcement.

2.note: Vote against 287(g) is the operational opposite of opposing sanctuary policy; rhetoric explicitly pro-sanctuary in posture.

profile.background: Born June 30 1964, Dayton OH; raised Albuquerque NM. BS Mechanical Engineering (U. New Mexico), MBA (Cornell 1989). Former U.S. State Department Foreign Service Officer (Mexico, South Africa) and Peace Corps Country Director (Belize, 2006). Miami-Dade County Commissioner District 5 Jun 2018-Nov 2025 (won 2018 special election after Bruno Barreiro resigned). Elected 44th Mayor of Miami Dec 9 2025 runoff (59.46% vs. Emilio Gonzalez 40.54%); sworn in Dec 18 2025; term to 2029. First woman Mayor of Miami; first non-Hispanic since 1996; first Democrat elected mayor since 1997.

eileen-higgins.notes_set: 44th Mayor of Miami (D); won Dec 9 2025 runoff 59.46% vs. Emilio Gonzalez 40.54%; sworn in Dec 18 2025; term to 2029. First woman Mayor of Miami; first non-Hispanic since 1996; first Democrat elected mayor since 1997. Former Miami-Dade County Commissioner District 5 (Jun 2018-Nov 2025; resigned to take mayoral office). Former U.S. State Dept FSO and Peace Corps Country Director (Belize). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement evidence-pass] HARD evidence (county tenure + mayoral campaign): SPONSORED 2019 Miami-Dade ordinance creating county's first codified LGBTQ Advisory Board (incl. explicit trans rep); declared countywide Transgender Day of Visibility 2024; multi-year Pride proclamation/flag-raising history at County Hall — biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE and family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE. HARD evidence on guns: longtime Moms Demand Action volunteer; Giffords-endorsed; advocated background-check expansion and against gun shows on county property — self_defense q0/q1 FALSE. HARD evidence on immigration: publicly opposes Miami's 287(g) cooperation, vowed to minimize Miami PD ICE involvement, called Alligator Alcatraz 'a sin'; quoted: 'There's no reason police in the city of Miami should be doing the job of federal law enforcement' — border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE and refuse_state_overreach q0 TRUE (asserted local control against state ICE mandate, content-neutral on direction). NO direct quote located on personhood-from-conception or abortion-abolition (Dem-coalition endorsements are indirect — held null per evidence-first discipline). NO documented position on paper ballots / voter ID / Zuckerbucks specifically — null. Religion: Catholic per secondary biographical aggregators; not confirmed via her own statement — left null in profile.

0.note: Sponsored Oct 2019 Miami-Dade ordinance creating county's first codified LGBTQ Advisory Board (explicitly includes trans representation); regular sponsor of Pride flag-raisings at County Hall.

2.note: Declared countywide Transgender Day of Visibility for Mar 31 2024; opposes FL laws restricting transgender care.

4.note: Co-sponsored Pride flag raising at Miami-Dade County Hall and LGBTQ History Month designations — institutional promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

2.note: Sponsorship of LGBTQ Advisory Board with trans representation + public opposition to FL transgender-care restrictions runs counter to opposing SOGI promotion in policy/schools.

0.note: Publicly asserted local control against state ICE-cooperation mandate; said Miami should follow South Miami's lawsuit against state preemption: 'There's no reason police in the city of Miami should be doing the job of federal law enforcement.' Content-neutral assertion of local autonomy.

1.note: Opposed Miami's 287(g) participation; called for restoration of Venezuelan TPS; condemned 'Alligator Alcatraz' detention facility as 'a sin.' Quoted: 'We have to follow the law, but we're not helping if we don't have to.'

2.note: Publicly opposes 287(g) ICE cooperation; vows to minimize Miami PD's ICE involvement — functional sanctuary posture within FL preemption limits.

0.note: Longtime Moms Demand Action volunteer; Giffords-endorsed; advocated for 'comprehensive background and mental health screenings' — record incompatible with constitutional-carry-without-permits.

1.note: Moms Demand Action / Giffords alignment includes support for red-flag laws, AWB, mag limits as standard organizational positions she partnered with.

set.office: Mayor of Miami (2025 R nominee · LOST Dec 9 2025 runoff 40.5% to Higgins 59.5%; retired US Army colonel; former Miami City Manager 2018-2020)

profile.background: Retired U.S. Army colonel (26 years). Director, U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services under President George W. Bush (Dec 2005-Apr 2008). CEO, Miami International Airport (2013-2017). Miami City Manager (Jan 2018-Feb 2020, under Mayor Francis Suarez). PhD International Relations, U. Miami. Cuban-born, raised Tampa. Ran for Miami Mayor in 2025 with endorsements from Trump, DeSantis, Cruz, Rick Scott; successfully sued the Miami City Commission to overturn the June 2025 ordinance postponing the election, winning at the 11th Judicial Circuit and the FL 3rd DCA; election proceeded Nov 4 + Dec 9 2025 (runoff). LOST runoff to Democrat Eileen Higgins 40.54% (15,099) to 59.46% (22,145). No current elected office.

emilio-gonzalez-mayor.notes_set: RECORD CORRECTED 2026-06: Emilio Gonzalez LOST the Dec 9 2025 Miami mayoral runoff to Eileen Higgins (D) 40.54% to 59.46% — prior record incorrectly tagged 'won Nov 2025' and credited his USCIS Director service to Trump (was actually under George W. Bush, Dec 2005-Apr 2008). Status set to 'lost'; candidacy_status set to 'lost.' Next Miami mayoral election: 2029. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement evidence-pass] Civic-defining act: successfully SUED the incumbent-controlled Miami City Commission to overturn the June 2025 ordinance postponing the city election, winning at the 11th Judicial Circuit and the FL 3rd DCA (strong election-integrity / anti-self-dealing record; not directly on rubric q0/q1/q4 — captured here in notes). HARD evidence from campaign: (a) attacked 'out of control' city budget growth ($1.5B at his City Manager exit to $3B now), pledged Day-One audit and homestead property-tax cut, backed DeSantis property-tax phase-out — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE; (b) endorsed by five former MPD/MDPD officials and six former police chiefs, pledged to 'increase police presence,' called police/firefighters 'the real public servants' — public_justice q0 TRUE; (c) clear anti-sanctuary framing (former USCIS Director, debate-stage quote: 'I cannot in good conscience fight the federal government and defend a rapist or a murderer, like happens in other cities') — border_immigration q2 TRUE; BUT (d) explicitly NARROWED deportation support to those who 'commit crimes' / 'rapists, thugs, murderers and child molesters,' deflecting on non-violent illegal aliens to 'a federal issue' — fails the rubric's q1 mandatory-deportation-of-ALL standard, scored FALSE. NOTABLE DEVIATIONS from R-archetype: accepted Log Cabin Republicans of Miami endorsement with no public counter-statement on social/family/marriage/abortion issues; ran a fiscal/competence/anti-corruption campaign, NOT a social-conservative one — all life/marriage/family/Christian-liberty/2A cells held null absent specific documented statements.

2.note: Campaign kickoff attacked 'out of control' Miami budget growth from $1.5B to $3B; pledged Day-One audit, homestead property-tax cut, and backed DeSantis property-tax phase-out — affirmative documented anti-debt/fiscal-restraint posture.

0.note: Endorsed by five former MPD/MDPD officials and six former police chiefs (Colina, Llanes, Orza, Perez, etc.); pledged to 'increase police presence' and called police/firefighters 'the real public servants' — meets the affirmative-advocacy HIGH BAR.

1.note: Explicitly QUALIFIED his deportation support to those who 'commit crimes' / 'rapists, thugs, murderers and child molesters,' deflecting on non-violent illegal aliens. Statement: 'I will always defend my immigrant community. But I will never ever defend rapists, thugs, murderers and child molesters just to make a point with the federal government.' Narrower than the rubric's q1 mandatory-deportation-of-ALL standard.

2.note: Former USCIS Director; on debate stage said 'I cannot in good conscience fight the federal government and defend a rapist or a murderer, like happens in other cities' — clear anti-sanctuary framing.
